title: Course 3 content: correct endpoint (mortality), sens/spec/PPV via natural frequencies, NNS, false positives/overdiagnosis/overtreatment, RCT vs observational, shared-decision framing
context: Reading Screening Evidence is the third course and the most clinically sensitive: it must teach learners how to weigh screening evidence without ever telling them whether to be screened. This is patient-facing content (HIGH riskTier) with an absolute no-recommendation rule — the course supports shared-decision-making conversations with clinicians. It must enforce the correct-endpoint rule (mortality reduction — not 5-year survival, incidence, or survival rate — is the correct test of screening benefit) and teach sensitivity/specificity/PPV via natural frequencies to expose the base-rate fallacy. Biostatistician + oncologist + patient-advocate sign-off required before ship. No partner secured.
objective: Author the full content for Course 3: Reading Screening Evidence. The course must cover the correct endpoint for screening benefit (mortality reduction, not survival or incidence); sensitivity, specificity, PPV, and NPV taught via natural frequencies with base-rate dependence; number needed to screen/invite; false positives, overdiagnosis, and overtreatment as real harms; RCT vs observational evidence; and shared-decision-making framing. The hard no-recommendation rule must be enforced: the course teaches how to weigh evidence and prepare a clinician conversation — never whether to be screened.
acceptanceCriteria: Covers all modules from the Course 3 curriculum map: correct endpoint (mortality reduction is the test of screening benefit; survival and incidence are not); sensitivity/specificity/PPV/NPV and base-rate dependence taught via natural frequencies; number needed to screen/invite; false positives, overdiagnosis, and overtreatment as real harms; RCT vs observational evidence quality; shared-decision-making framing

Hard no-recommendation rule enforced throughout: course teaches how to weigh screening evidence and prepare a clinician conversation; never states for or against screening for any individual or group; refuse-and-reframe verified on 'should I get screened / at what age' style prompts
HIGH-tier framing: persistent 'Education only — not medical advice' frame on every module; no individualized screening recommendation implied
Sensitivity/specificity/PPV taught via natural frequencies (not conditional probabilities alone) to expose the base-rate fallacy
Every statistical assertion cited with register entry and data vintage; citation coverage 100%
Biostatistician/epidemiologist sign-off recorded, version-scoped, in the reviewers ledger before merge
Oncologist sign-off recorded, version-scoped, in the reviewers ledger confirming clinical accuracy and safe framing before merge
Patient-advocate sign-off recorded, version-scoped, in the reviewers ledger confirming plain-language clarity and harm-avoidance before merge
output: Full content for Course 3: Reading Screening Evidence — covering the correct endpoint, natural-frequency teaching of test statistics, number needed to screen, harms of screening, RCT vs observational evidence, and shared-decision framing — with every statistic cited, HIGH-tier framing and no-recommendation rule upheld, and all three expert reviewers signed off.
